eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
emote: verb. To display or express (emotions, mental states, etc.) openly, particularly while acting, and especially in an excessive manner.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
eve: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Even.
event: noun. An occurrence; something that happens.
meet: verb. To make contact (with someone) while in proximity.
mem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mangala.

memo: noun. A short note; a memorandum.

mete: verb. To dispense, measure in order to dispense, allot (especially punishment, reward etc.).

mome: noun. A dull or stupid person.
moment: noun. A particular point, or relatively small region.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.

mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.

move: verb. To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
movement: noun. Physical motion between points in space.

net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nocamán.

omen: noun. Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teem: verb. To be stocked to overflowing.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
tome: noun. One in a series of volumes.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
toneme: noun. A phoneme in a language that uses different tones for different meanings.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
venom: noun. An animal toxin intended for defensive or offensive use; a biological poison delivered by bite, sting, etc., to protect an animal or
vent: noun. An opening through which gases, especially air, can pass.
vet: noun. A veterinarian or veterinary surgeon.
veto: noun. A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vote: noun. A formalized choice on legally relevant measures such as employment or appointment to office or a proceeding about a legal dispute.
